changes_made: Renamed k8s_admission_deny_latest_image_tag to k8s_image_latest_tag_prohibited for consistency with naming patterns
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to container image security. The naming convention was mostly followed, but one function required renaming for consistency. The control is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves image scanning and policies that can be enforced at the cluster level.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names use the 'image' resource prefix when related to container image security
title: Container Image Security
step1_reasoning: The control 'Container Image Security' focuses on ensuring that container images are secure before being deployed. In Kubernetes, this can be achieved through image scanning on admission, enforcing image pull policies, enabling vulnerability scanning, and avoiding the use of 'latest' tags. These checks align with the cloud security checks for image scanning and encryption in container registries.
